heading: Prioritization of efforts and assistance for energy infrastructure projects in Europe and Eurasia
label: 9563 Prioritization of efforts and assistance for energy infrastructure projects in Europe and Eurasia
notes: References in TextThe BUILD Act, referred to in subsec. (e), probably means the BUILD Act of 2018, also known as the Better Utilization of Investments Leading to Development Act of 2018, which is classified principally to chapter 103 (§ 9601 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.section 9601 of this title

Delegation of Authority Under the European Energy Security and Diversification Act of 2019Memorandum of President of the United States, , 87 F.R. 35853, provided:June 8, 2022Memorandum for the Secretary of StateBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2004(e)(1)(C) of the European Energy Security and Diversification Act of 2019 () (the “Act”). The delegation in this memorandum shall apply to any provision of any future public law that is the same or substantially the same as the provision referenced in this memorandum. Any reference in this memorandum to the Act shall be deemed to be a reference to such Act as amended from time to time.section 301 of title 322 U.S.C. 9563(e)(1)(C)You are authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.J.R. Biden, Jr.

children.label: A
children.text: to facilitate international negotiations concerning cross-border infrastructure;

children.label: B
children.text: to enhance Europe’s regulatory environment with respect to energy; and

children.label: C
children.text: to develop accessible, transparent, and competitive energy markets supplied by diverse sources, types, and routes of energy; and

children.label: A
children.text: to diversify the energy sources and supply routes of European and Eurasian countries;

children.label: B
children.text: to enhance energy market integration across the region; and

children.label: C
children.text: to increase competition within energy markets.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The agencies described in subsection (a) shall identify energy infrastructure projects that would be appropriate for United States assistance under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: improves electricity transmission infrastructure, power generation through the use of a broad power mix (including fossil fuel and renewable energy), or energy efficiency; or

children.label: ii
children.text: advances electricity storage projects, smart grid projects, distributed generation models, or other technological innovations, as appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: is located in a European or Eurasian country.

children.label: A
children.text: link the energy systems of 2 or more European or Eurasian countries;

children.label: B
children.text: have already been identified by the European Commission as being integral for the energy security of European countries;

children.label: C
children.text: are expected to enhance energy market integration;

children.label: D
children.text: can attract funding from the private sector, an international financial institution, the government of the country in which the project will be carried out, or the European Commission; or

children.label: E
children.text: have the potential to use United States goods and services during project implementation.

children.heading: Diplomatic and political support
children.label: 1 Diplomatic and political support
children.text: The Secretary of State shall provide diplomatic and political support to the European Commission and the governments of European and Eurasian countries, as necessary, including by using the diplomatic and political influence and expertise of the Department of State to build the capacity of those countries to resolve any impediments to the development of projects selected under subsection (b).

children.heading: Early-stage project support
children.label: 2 Early-stage project support
children.text: The Director of the Trade and Development Agency shall provide early-stage project support with respect to projects selected under subsection (b), as necessary.

children.heading: Late-stage project support
children.label: 3 Late-stage project support
children.text: Agencies described in subsection (a) that provide late-stage project support shall do so with respect to projects selected under subsection (b), as necessary.

children.heading: Exception from certain limitation under BUILD Act
children.label: e Exception from certain limitation under BUILD Act
A
children.text: the United States International Development Finance Corporation may provide support for projects in countries with upper-middle-income economies or high-income economies (as those terms are defined by the World Bank);

children.label: B
children.text: the restriction under shall not apply; andsection 9612(c)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the President certifies to the appropriate congressional committees that such support furthers the national economic or foreign policy interests of the United States; and

children.label: I
children.text: designed to produce significant developmental outcomes or provide developmental benefits to the poorest population of that country; or

children.label: II
children.text: necessary to preempt or counter efforts by a strategic competitor of the United States to secure significant political or economic leverage or acquire national security-sensitive technologies or infrastructure in a country that is an ally or partner of the United States.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 2 Definitions
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “appropriate congressional committees” and “less developed country” have the meanings given those terms in .section 9601 of this title
